title: Alarm on CloudWatch metrics with the right statistic and period
description: Choose statistic, period, and treatMissingData deliberately, use metric math for ratios, and emit structured EMF logs instead of scraping log text.
body: Use when adding observability to an AWS workload or wiring an alarm. Do not alarm on Average when you need p99, and avoid treatMissingData:missing on a metric that goes silent during the very failure you care about. Actions: select the statistic (p99 via extended stats) and period that matches the SLO, use metric math (errors/requests) for ratio alarms, set treatMissingData explicitly, and publish custom metrics through Embedded Metric Format so cardinality stays controlled. Validation signals include the alarm flipping OK->ALARM on an injected fault, returning to OK on recovery, and dashboards reflecting the EMF dimensions. Risk boundary: deleting or silencing a production alarm is human-gated.
